All routine investigations – pelvic ultrasound, urine and vaginal cultures, cystoscopy – are normal, and there is no vaginal discharge.

These findings are classic for bladder pain syndrome/interstitial cystitis, a condition that presents with pelvic pain and lower urinary tract symptoms in the absence of infection or obvious structural abnormalities.
The normal cystoscopy does not rule it out, as many patients have a normal or only subtle cystoscopic appearance.
Therefore, the most likely diagnosis is interstitial cystitis (painful bladder syndrome).
